text: in the reference implemenation there is this beautiful idea that arrows are as large as the value that flows from the supported aim to the supporting
description:
let's restore that. 
Meaning, if an aim supports only 1 parent and has no intrinsic value, the connection width will be equal to the radius. 
Maybe calculations also need to store calculated flow values (normalized, too)

status.comment: Verified implementation in GraphLink.vue. Arrow width is calculated as `intoR * share`, which means it scales with the value flow share relative to the target radius. Matches requirement.
